lines.text: Welcome back. The clock reads {{CLOCK}}. We are {{ELAPSED}} into the night.
I'm Sister Marigold. For the next hour, we breathe together.
Find a place in your home where you feel safe. It doesn't have to be comfortable. It has to be yours.
[pause] Breathe in through your nose. Count to four. One. Two. Three. Four.
Hold it. Feel the air in your chest. You chose to stay inside. That was a choice.
Now release. Through your mouth. Slowly. Let it out.
[pause] Again. In. Four counts. The people on Atwood Avenue right now — they are doing this too. You're not alone in the darkness.
Hold. [pause] Your neighbor might be listening to this same broadcast. The one two houses down. Or three blocks over on Tenney Park.
Release.
[pause] Scan your body now. Start at your forehead. Is it tight? Most of you — yes. That's the midnight hour. That's what midnight does.
Let it soften. Imagine the tension running down like water. Down your face. Your neck.
Your shoulders are carrying something. Everyone's shoulders are carrying something tonight. Set it down for four minutes.
[pause] The Willy Street neighborhood watch is still awake. Tucker confirmed it earlier. They are holding a perimeter.
That is someone else's choice. Your choice is this room. This breath. In.
Four counts. [pause] Feel your chest rise.
Hold.
Now scan down. Your heart. It's beating fast maybe. That's normal. The body doesn't understand the difference between fear and waiting.
Your body thinks something is coming. But nothing is coming through your locked doors. You locked them. You know they are locked.
Release. [pause] Breathe.
Stomach. Belly. Relax it. Most of you are holding everything here. In the gut. Years of this. Years of one night a year turning into what it turns into.
[pause] You have survived every Purge before this one. Let that be true in your body.
In. Four counts.
Hold. Feel the relief of the oxygen.
The East Side is loud right now. We know that. Tucker reported it. But you are not on the East Side. You are where you chose to be.
Release.
[pause] Now move to your legs. Your thighs. Are they tense? They probably are. Runners — cyclists — they hold it here.
But you're not running tonight. You're sitting. You're safe. Let the muscles believe that.
In. [pause] Four. Hold. Release.
Feet. The ground. If you're sitting, feel your feet on the floor. If you're standing, feel the earth below you — and I mean that literally and also not.
[pause] The Sisters say that the earth remembers. It forgets violence faster than we do.
In. Four. Hold. Release.
[pause] Now picture the room you are in. Really see it. The shadows. The light from outside — or no light if you've covered the windows.
Most of you have covered the windows. That's good. That's a choice. That's protective.
But right now — just in your mind — imagine uncovering them for one second. Just to see what Madison looks like at {{CLOCK}}.
What do you see? [pause] Most of you see darkness. Some of you see other houses with covered windows. Some of you see lights you know aren't power lights.
Now cover them again. In your mind. You're safe. In. Four. Hold.
[pause] The ones outside are not your responsibility. Say that to yourself. The ones outside made their choice.
You made yours. In. Release.
{{ELAPSED}} have passed. Seven hours remain. That's not a long time when you're breathing together with forty thousand other people.
That's actually a very long time. But time doesn't move the same way on Purge night. You know this.
[pause] In. Four. Hold. [pause] Do you have anything in this room you regret? Anything you wish you'd said differently?
Most of you do. Carry it with you. Don't set it down. Let it remind you that you're still here. That you still have time.
Release. [pause] In.
The Quiet Order teaches that forgiveness is not about the people you forgive. It's about the space you make in yourself. For breath. For next year.
I'm not telling you to forgive anyone outside. I'm telling you to forgive yourself for being frightened. For being ordinary. For surviving.
In. Four. Hold. [pause] Ashta-kala-something-something. [pause] Release.
The light will come. It always comes. And when it does, you'll still be here. That's what matters.
[pause] Breathe with me until I'm gone. In. Four. Hold. Release. [pause] You're doing beautifully.

tickerItems.text: East Side (Atwood) reports +8 kill count spike 22:30–23:00. Concentrated near the corridor.
Willy Street neighborhood watch perimeter holding at Yahara Bridge. Armed positions maintained.
The earth remembers. It forgets violence faster than we do.
Text from Tenney Park listener: 'Thank you for reminding me the doors are locked.'
You have survived every Purge before this one. Let that be true in your body.
